Dong Jun Kim

AI and LLM Interpretability Researcher

Hi, I'm Jun, a researcher at Korea University with a deep passion for unraveling the inner workings of Large Language Models (LLMs). My research focuses on mechanistic interpretability, where I aim to reverse-engineer the algorithms and structures within LLMs to better understand how they process information and make decisions. By shedding light on these intricate systems, I strive to enhance their transparency, reliability, and alignment with human values.

I am driven by an insatiable curiosity to understand the unknown. My ultimate goal is to demystify the architecture of Deep Neural Networks and contribute to building AI systems that are not only powerful but also ethically sound and safe. I see my work as part of a broader mission to ensure that advanced AI technologies are developed responsibly and aligned with long-term societal interests.

I take pride in being at the cutting edge of AI research, constantly pushing boundaries to uncover new insights into LLMs and their behavior. I am committed to becoming a leading researcher in this field, contributing both theoretical advancements and practical solutions.

If you share my enthusiasm for understanding the mechanisms behind LLMs or are interested in AI safety and interpretability, feel free to connect—I'm always eager to exchange ideas and collaborate.

Research Interests

Mechanistic Interpretability

Reverse-engineering LLMs to uncover their internal circuits, algorithms, and decision-making processes.

AI Safety

Developing frameworks to ensure advanced AI systems are robust, aligned with human values, and operate safely under all conditions.

Mechanistic Anomaly Detection

Identifying unexpected or harmful behaviors in LLMs by analyzing their internal mechanisms.

LLM Architectures

Investigating innovative designs and structural insights that improve model performance while enhancing interpretability.

Sparse Autoencoders & Transformer Circuits

Exploring sparsity in neural networks and its role in improving efficiency and functionality in LLMs.

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

Enhancing LLM outputs by integrating retrieval-based methods for more accurate and grounded responses.

Bias & Harm Mitigation

Addressing ethical challenges by detecting, understanding, and mitigating biases in AI systems.

LLM Alignment

Ensuring language models align with human intentions while maintaining high performance and reliability.

Education and Research Journey

Over the years, my journey has evolved from building digital twins with HPC techniques to exploring AR/VR innovations and now pioneering research in LLM interpretability and AI safety. Each step has been driven by an insatiable curiosity to understand complex systems and a desire to contribute meaningfully to the future of AI.

2024 – Present

Korea University, Seoul, South Korea

Currently pursuing my Master's in Computer Science at Korea University under the guidance of heui-seok lim at NLP&AI Lab, I focused on the interpretability and safety of Large Language Models (LLMs). My research centers on understanding the inner mechanisms of LLMs, developing frameworks for their ethical deployment, and advancing methods for model transparency and explainability. As part of an interdisciplinary team, I lead projects aimed at extending AI applications while ensuring alignment with human values. My work reflects a deep commitment to unraveling the complexities of LLM architectures and bridging the gap between cutting-edge AI research and responsible development.

2019 – 2023

University of South Florida, Tampa, FL

I completed my Bachelor of Science in Computer Science, graduating cum laude. During my time there, I dove deep into the foundations of computer science, with coursework in Machine Learning, Data Structures, and Algorithms. My passion for computational modeling and simulation led me to join Dr. Edwin Michael's lab, where I worked on high-performance computing (HPC) projects. I collaborated with Hillsborough County to build a city-scale digital twin, implementing agent-based models and leveraging tools like MPI, OpenMP, and CUDA for parallel computing. This experience sparked my initial fascination with modeling complex systems and their real-world applications.

Recent Publications

CitySEIRCast: an agent-based city digital twin for pandemic analysis and simulation

Bilal, S., Zaatour, W., Alonso Otano, Y., Saha, A., Newcomb, K., Kim, S., Kim, J., Ginjala, R., Groen, D., Michael, E. Complex & Intelligent Systems, 11(1), pp. 1-29, 2025.

Read Paper

Exploring Coding Spot: Understanding Parametric Contributions to LLM Coding Performance

Kim, D., Kim, M., Chun, Y., Park, C., Lim, H. arXiv preprint arXiv:2412.07113, 2024.

Read Paper

A View Direction-Driven Approach for Automatic Room Mapping in Mixed Reality

Kim, Dong Jun and Wanwan Li. Proceedings of the 2023 5th International Conference on Image Processing and Machine Vision, pp. 29–33, 2023.

Read Paper

Exploring Inherent Biases in LLMs within Korean Social Context: A Comparative Analysis of ChatGPT and GPT-4

Lee, Seungyoon, Dong Kim, Dahyun Jung, Chanjun Park, and Heui-Seok Lim. Proceedings of the 2024 Conference of the North American Chapter of the Association for Computational Linguistics: Human Language Technologies, pp. 93–104, 2024.
